description: Visualizations of the machine learning stack around quantum-pipeline project.

panels.title: VQE Efficiency
panels.description: Ratio of VQE optimization time to total run time. Higher means less overhead from setup (Hamiltonian generation, qubit mapping).

panels.title: Overhead Ratio
panels.description: Non-VQE time (Hamiltonian + mapping) divided by VQE optimization time. Lower is better - values above 1.0 mean setup dominates compute.

panels.title: Iterations per Second
panels.description: VQE optimizer iterations completed per second. Depends on qubit count, backend (CPU/GPU), and optimizer type. Useful for comparing hardware lanes.

panels.title: Setup Ratio
panels.description: Fraction of total time spent on setup (Hamiltonian generation + qubit mapping) vs actual VQE optimization.

panels.title: VQE Total Time
panels.description: Wall-clock time for the entire VQE pipeline: Hamiltonian generation, qubit mapping, and optimization loop.

panels.title: Time per Iteration
panels.description: Average time per optimizer iteration. Scales with qubit count due to larger statevector simulation. Compare across backends to measure GPU speedup.

panels.title: Iteration Count
panels.description: Total optimizer iterations before convergence or max limit. Random init typically needs more iterations than HF init. L-BFGS-B uses extra evaluations for gradient computation.

panels.title: Pending
panels.description: Number of experiment runs waiting to be processed across all lanes.

panels.title: Progress
panels.description: Percentage of total experiment runs completed for the selected tier.

panels.title: Throughput (runs/hour)
panels.description: Rate of completed experiment runs per hour, per lane. Extrapolated from the last 10 minutes.

panels.title: Failed Over Time
panels.description: Cumulative count of failed runs per lane. Includes timeouts and non-zero exit codes.

panels.title: Done
panels.description: Total number of successfully completed experiment runs for the selected tier.

panels.title: Failed
panels.description: Total number of failed experiment runs (timeouts + errors) for the selected tier.

panels.title: Per-Lane Progress
panels.description: Completed runs per hardware lane (GPU1, GPU2, CPU). Each lane processes molecules sequentially, one per invocation.

panels.title: Accuracy Score
panels.description: Log-scale score (0-100) measuring VQE energy vs Hartree-Fock baseline. Higher = closer to HF. Useful for comparing optimizers and init strategies on the same molecule. Score of ~94 means 1 mHa difference from HF.

panels.title: Reference Energy
panels.description: Hartree-Fock (HF) energy computed by PySCF for the same molecule and basis set. This is the mean-field baseline that VQE aims to improve upon by capturing electron correlation.

panels.title: Ground State Energy
panels.description: VQE-computed ground state energy (electronic + nuclear repulsion). Should be at or below the HF reference for a well-converged run.

panels.title: Energy Error (mHa)
panels.description: Difference between VQE energy and HF reference in millihartree. Negative values mean VQE found lower energy than HF (good - correlation energy captured). Large positive values indicate poor convergence.

panels.title: GPU Utilization
panels.description: GPU compute core utilization. VQE shows spiky patterns (bursts during circuit simulation, idle during optimizer steps). Higher sustained utilization expected with larger qubit counts.

panels.title: GPU Power Draw
panels.description: GPU power consumption in watts. Correlates with compute load. Baseline idle power ~10-15W for Pascal GPUs.

panels.title: Fan Speed
panels.description: GPU fan speed as percentage of maximum. Increases with temperature to maintain thermal limits.

panels.title: Power State
panels.description: NVIDIA P-state (0 = max performance, 8 = idle). Lower values indicate active GPU compute.

panels.title: Graphics Clock (MHz)
panels.description: GPU core clock frequency. Boost clocks activate under load. Drops during idle periods between VQE iterations.

panels.title: Memory Free
panels.description: Available GPU VRAM. Statevector simulation memory scales as 2^(qubits+4) bytes. 18-qubit molecule needs ~4 MB.

panels.title: GPU Temperature
panels.description: GPU die temperature. Pascal GPUs throttle around 83-94C. Normal VQE workloads stay well below this.

panels.title: GPU Memory Usage
panels.description: Percentage of GPU VRAM in use. Low values (1-5%) are expected for small molecules. Increases significantly at 20+ qubits.

panels.title: CPU Usage
panels.description: Host CPU utilization per container type. Classical optimizers (L-BFGS-B, COBYLA) run entirely on CPU between GPU circuit evaluations.

panels.title: Memory Usage
panels.description: Host RAM usage per container type. Includes Python runtime, Qiskit data structures, and statevector copies.

panels.title: Uptime
panels.description: Time since the monitoring process started for each container type.

panels.title: Load Average (1m)
panels.description: 1-minute system load average. Values above CPU core count indicate saturation. With 3 parallel lanes on a 6-core CPU, expect values near 6.

panels.title: Redpanda Input Latency (ms)
panels.description: Average time for Redpanda Connect to read messages from the Kafka source topic.

panels.title: Redpanda Batch Throughput
panels.description: Rate of message batches received and sent by Redpanda Connect. Sent/received should track closely.

panels.title: Redpanda Output Latency (ms)
panels.description: Average time for Redpanda Connect to write processed messages to the sink (Iceberg/MinIO).

panels.title: Redpanda Errors/sec
panels.description: Rate of output errors in Redpanda Connect. Non-zero indicates connectivity or serialization issues.

panels.title: Redpanda Output Connection
panels.description: Output sink connection status (1 = connected, 0 = disconnected).

panels.title: Redpanda Input Connection
panels.description: Input source connection status (1 = connected, 0 = disconnected).

panels.title: Redpanda Messages In/sec
panels.description: Rate of individual messages consumed from the Kafka source topic.

panels.title: Redpanda Messages Out/sec
panels.description: Rate of individual messages written to the output sink.

panels.title: Redis Exporter Up
panels.description: Redis exporter health (1 = scraping successfully).

panels.title: Postgres Exporter Up
panels.description: Postgres exporter health (1 = scraping successfully).

panels.title: Redis Keys
panels.description: Total number of keys stored in Redis db0. Tracks cached state size.

panels.title: Redis Evicted Keys
panels.description: Total keys evicted due to memory pressure. Non-zero may indicate Redis needs more memory.

panels.title: Postgres Connections
panels.description: Active backend connections per database (Airflow, MLflow). Watch for connection pool exhaustion.

panels.title: Redis Network I/O
panels.description: Redis network throughput (bytes/sec). Spikes correlate with cache reads/writes during pipeline runs.

panels.title: Postgres Deadlocks
panels.description: Cumulative deadlock count per database. Should be zero in normal operation.

panels.title: Redis Uptime
panels.description: Seconds since Redis server started. Resets indicate restarts.

panels.title: Postgres Cache Hit Ratio
panels.description: Buffer cache hit ratio. Values above 0.95 are healthy. Low ratios mean excessive disk reads.

panels.title: Postgres Active Queries
panels.description: Currently executing queries across all databases.

panels.title: Postgres Locks
panels.description: Total active locks. High counts during batch generation are normal (Airflow state updates).

panels.title: Postgres Tuple Operations/sec
panels.description: Rate of row inserts, updates, and deletes per database. Shows write activity patterns.

panels.title: Redis Clients
panels.description: Connected and blocked Redis clients. Blocked clients may indicate contention.

panels.title: Postgres Transactions/sec
panels.description: Rate of committed and rolled-back transactions. Rollbacks indicate failed operations.

panels.title: Redis Hit Rate
panels.description: Cache hit ratio (hits / (hits + misses)). Low values suggest cache is not effective for the workload.

panels.title: Redis Ops/sec
panels.description: Total Redis commands processed per second.

panels.title: Postgres DB Size
panels.description: On-disk size of each database. Grows with Airflow metadata and MLflow experiment tracking.

panels.title: Redis Memory
panels.description: Redis memory usage: current, RSS (OS-reported), and peak. RSS exceeding allocated memory indicates fragmentation.

panels.title: Garage Disk Usage
panels.description: Percentage of local disk used by the Garage S3-compatible object store (MinIO alternative).

panels.title: Garage Stored Blocks
panels.description: Total data blocks stored in Garage. Grows as VQE results and Iceberg data files are written.

panels.title: Garage Cluster Health
panels.description: Overall cluster health status (1 = healthy). Unhealthy indicates node or replication issues.

panels.title: Garage Connected Nodes
panels.description: Number of Garage nodes currently reachable in the cluster.

panels.title: Garage Storage Nodes
panels.description: Number of storage nodes reporting OK status.

panels.title: Garage Partitions OK
panels.description: Number of data partitions with all replicas available. Should equal total partitions.

panels.title: Garage Resync Queue
panels.description: Pending block resyncs and errored blocks. Non-zero queue is normal after writes; persistent errors need investigation.

panels.title: Garage Admin Request Rate
panels.description: Rate of admin API requests to Garage. Spikes during bucket operations or health checks.

panels.title: DAGBag Size
panels.description: Number of DAGs loaded by the Airflow scheduler. Includes VQE batch generation, feature extraction, and sync DAGs.

panels.title: Airflow Task Instances
panels.description: Currently running task instances and creation rate. Shows pipeline execution activity.

panels.title: Airflow DAG Parse Time
panels.description: Total time for the scheduler to parse all DAG files. High values slow down scheduling.

panels.title: Scheduler Executable Tasks
panels.description: Tasks ready to be scheduled. Persistent non-zero values indicate executor bottleneck.

panels.title: Triggerer Capacity
panels.description: Available triggerer slots and currently running triggers. Used for deferred/async task execution.

panels.title: Airflow Executor Tasks
panels.description: Running, queued, and available executor slots. Shows scheduler capacity and utilization.

panels.title: Airflow Scheduler Heartbeat
panels.description: Scheduler and DAG processor heartbeat rates. Drops indicate scheduler health issues.

panels.title: DAG Import Errors
panels.description: Number of DAG files that failed to parse. Non-zero means broken DAG definitions.

panels.title: Airflow Pool Slots (default_pool)
panels.description: Default pool slot allocation: running, queued, and open. Controls max parallel task execution.

title: Quantum Pipeline - ML Stack
